  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはイエス・キリストの生涯における初期のエピソードです。",
      "それは2月2日に多くの教会で祝われる祝祭です。",
      "それはクリスマスの40日後のカンドゥルマスに祝われる祝祭です。",
      "それは「イエスの奉献の祝日」と呼ばれる祝祭です。",
      "それは新約聖書のルカによる福音書の第2章に記されています。",
      "それは東方正教会における12の大祝祭の一つとして祝われます。",
      "それは東方正教会ではヒパパンテと呼ばれることもあります。",
      "それは西方キリスト教では主の奉献の祝日として知られています。",
      "それは西方キリスト教では処女の清めの祝日という以前の名前で知られています。",
      "それは西方キリスト教では主の出会いという以前の名前で知られています。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それはビザンチン伝承において、主の大祝祭と神の母の大祝祭の両方の要素を兼ね備えています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは清めの儀式とユダヤ人の長子贖罪の儀式を組み合わせたものとして特徴づけられます。",
      "それはこの日のミサでの灯火行列によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはシメオンによる「諸国民への光」の宣言によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは一年を通して使用される蜜蝋ろうそくの祝福によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは信徒の誓願の更新によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは義人シメオンの預言によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは天の父の家の中でイエスが現れた最初の顕現として特徴づけられます。",
      "それは蜜蝋ろうそくの祝福によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはユダヤの律法に従ったマリアの清めによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは長子の贖罪によって特徴づけられます。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは出産後のマリアの儀式的な清めを完了するために使用されます。",
      "それは長子贖罪を行うために使用されます。",
      "それは一部の典礼暦でエピファニーの季節の終わりを示すために使用されます。",
      "それは信徒の誓願の更新のために使用されます。",
      "それは神の母とヨセフが幼子イエスを神殿に連れて行ったことを記念するために使用されます。",
      "それはポーランドで嵐を避けるために使用されます。",
      "それは蜜蝋ろうそくの祝福のために使用されます。",
      "それは神の奉献聖体礼儀の祝典のために使用されます。",
      "それはマリアの儀式的な清めのために使用されます。",
      "それは長子の贖罪のために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはエルサレムの神殿にあります。"
    ],

      "それはクリスマスの40日後に祝われることで知られています。",
      "それはカンドゥルマスと呼ばれることで知られています。",

      "それは長子贖罪についてです。",
      "それはシメオンが幼子イエスに出会うことについてです。",
      "それはシメオンの預言についてです。",
      "それはアンナがイエスについて神に賛美の祈りを捧げることについてです。",

IsA: It is an early episode in the life of Jesus Christ.
It is a feast celebrated by many churches on February 2nd.
It is a feast celebrated 40 days after Christmas on Candlemas.
It is a feast called the "Feast of the Presentation of Jesus".
It is described in chapter 2 of the Gospel of Luke in the New Testament.
It is celebrated as one of the twelve Great Feasts in the Eastern Orthodox Church.
It is sometimes called Hypapante in the Eastern Orthodox Church.
It is known in Western Christianity as the Feast of the Presentation of the Lord.
It is known in Western Christianity by its earlier name as the Feast of the Purification of the Virgin.
It is known in Western Christianity by its earlier name as the Meeting of the Lord.
HasA: It has elements of both a Great Feast of the Lord and a Great Feast of the Theotokos in the Byzantine tradition.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the combination of the purification rite with the Jewish ceremony of the redemption of the firstborn.
It is characterized by the procession with lighted candles at the Mass on this day.
It is characterized by Simeon's proclamation of "a light for revelation to the Gentiles".
It is characterized by the blessing of beeswax candles for use throughout the year.
It is characterized by the renewal of religious vows.
It is characterized by the prophecy of Simeon the Righteous.
It is characterized by the earliest manifestation of Jesus inside the house of his heavenly Father.
It is characterized by the blessing of beeswax candles.
It is characterized by the ritual purification of Mary according to Jewish law.
It is characterized by the redemption of the firstborn son.
UsedFor: It is used for completing Mary's ritual purification after childbirth.
It is used for performing the redemption of the firstborn son.
It is used for marking the end of the Epiphany season in some liturgical calendars.
It is used for the renewal of religious vows.
It is used for remembering the Theotokos and Joseph taking the infant Jesus to the Temple.
It is used for warding off storms in Poland.
It is used for the blessing of beeswax candles.
It is used for the celebration of the Divine Liturgy.
It is used for the ritual purification of Mary.
It is used for the redemption of the firstborn.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Temple in Jerusalem.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Gospel of Luke.
CausedBy: It is caused by the Gospel of Luke.
It is caused by Mosaic law.
It is caused by the Torah.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the purification rite.
It consists of the Jewish ceremony of the redemption of the firstborn.
It consists of Mary and Joseph taking the Infant Jesus to the Temple.
It consists of Simeon encountering the Infant Jesus.
It consists of Simeon uttering the Nunc Dimittis.
It consists of Simeon prophesying to Mary.
It consists of Anna offering prayers and praise to God for Jesus.
It consists of the blessing of beeswax candles.
It consists of the Divine Liturgy.
It consists of the Churching of Women.
PartOf: It is part of the life of Jesus Christ.
It is part of the New Testament.
It is part of the liturgical calendar.
It is part of the Rosary as the fourth Joyful Mystery.
It is part of the Christian year.
It is part of the Byzantine tradition.
MemberOf: It is a member of the twelve Great Feasts in the Eastern Orthodox Church.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the early life of Jesus Christ.
It belongs to the New Testament.
It belongs to the liturgical celebrations of many churches.
It belongs to the Eastern Orthodox Church.
It belongs to Western Christianity.
It belongs to the Roman Catholic Church.
It belongs to the Anglican Communion.
It belongs to the Lutheran Church.
It belongs to the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America.
It belongs to the Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod.
KnownFor: It is known for being celebrated on February 2nd.
It is known for being celebrated 40 days after Christmas.
It is known for being called Candlemas.
It is known for being called the Feast of the Purification of the Virgin.
It is known for being called the Meeting of the Lord.
It is known for the encounter with Simeon.
It is known for Simeon's prophecy.
It is known for the encounter with Anna.
It is known for the blessing of candles.
It is known for the Churching of Women.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Jesus Christ.
It is associated with the Temple in Jerusalem.
It is associated with the Gospel of Luke.
It is associated with Mary and Joseph.
It is associated with Simeon.
It is associated with Anna.
It is associated with Candlemas.
It is associated with the purification rite.
It is associated with the redemption of the firstborn.
It is associated with the Nunc Dimittis.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Temple in Jerusalem.
It occurred forty days after the birth of Jesus.
About: It is about the presentation of Jesus at the Temple in Jerusalem.
It is about Mary and Joseph completing ritual purification.
It is about the redemption of the firstborn son.
It is about Simeon encountering the Infant Jesus.
It is about Simeon's prophecy.
It is about Anna's praise to God for Jesus.
It is about the blessing of candles.
It is about the Churching of Women.
It is about the manifestation of Jesus as the messiah.
It is about the renewal of religious vows.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Mosaic law.
It was influenced by the Torah.
It was influenced by the Gospel of Luke.
It was influenced by Pope Gelasius I.
It was influenced by the Second Vatican Council.
Influenced: It influenced the custom of churching of women.
It influenced the date of Candlemas.
It influenced the practice of blessing beeswax candles.
It influenced the celebration of the Divine Liturgy.
It influenced the practice of taking babies to church on the fortieth day after birth.

IsA: それはイエス・キリストの生涯における初期のエピソードです。
それは2月2日に多くの教会で祝われる祝祭です。
それはクリスマスの40日後のカンドゥルマスに祝われる祝祭です。

それは西方キリスト教では主の出会いという以前の名前で知られています。
